timeline.title: Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India: Standing history in sequence
timeline.overview: A route through surviving sites, ordered from the oldest standing layer to the newest urban memory still visible today.
timeline.whyThisRouteWorks: The route keeps to sites that still exist, stay mappable on the ground, and form a coherent chronological walk through the city.
timeline.transitionLogic: Each stop advances the city through a visible change in patronage, form, or civic memory.

chapters.summary: It is the defining standing monument of Madurai and anchors the city’s historic temple-centered urban form.
chapters.kicker: Core temple city, south of the Vaigai

chapters.summary: It broadens the dossier beyond the Shaiva core and shows Madurai’s larger sacred geography.

warnings: Coverage is thinner for this exact lens, so this story uses a shorter validated route than usual.

feasibleStops.title: Meenakshi Amman Temple
feasibleStops.areaLabel: Core temple city, south of the Vaigai
feasibleStops.historicalSummary: Major surviving temple complex at the heart of Madurai’s sacred and urban identity; the present fabric reflects long building, rebuilding, and patronage across the medieval and early modern eras.
feasibleStops.whyItMatters: It is the defining standing monument of Madurai and anchors the city’s historic temple-centered urban form.

feasibleStops.title: Azhagar Kovil
feasibleStops.historicalSummary: Standing Vishnu temple associated with the wider Madurai sacred landscape, surviving as an important regional pilgrimage site outside the core city center.
feasibleStops.whyItMatters: It broadens the dossier beyond the Shaiva core and shows Madurai’s larger sacred geography.
